summary: Split the old nonmatching umbrella file `src/melee/ft/ft_0CEE.c` into four matching ftCommon item-parasol motion units: `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFall`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial`, and `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall`. The PR updated GALE01 splits and `configure.py` so the new units link as Matching, replaced broad `ft/ft_0CEE.h` dependencies with per-motion headers, moved parasol helpers into ftCommon inlines/types, and renamed Peach parasol item fields to clearer `parasol_gobj_0`/`parasol_gobj_1` typed as `Item_GObj*`. The bot reported +1472 matched-code bytes and +1828 linked-code bytes, with 22 new matches in the new units and the expected broken matches in the deleted old unit.
what_changed: Replaced one GALE01 split entry for `melee/ft/ft_0CEE.c` covering text `0x800CEE70..0x800CF594` with four ftCommon item-parasol split entries: Open, Fall, FallSpecial, and DamageFall, each with its own `.text` and `.sdata2` ranges.
Changed `configure.py` from `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_0CEE.c")` to four `Object(Matching, ...)` entries for the new item-parasol units.
Deleted `src/melee/ft/ft_0CEE.c` and `src/melee/ft/ft_0CEE.h`, then moved/developed the parasol motion callbacks and entry functions into purpose-named ftCommon source/header pairs.

Added matching implementations for `ftCo_800CEE70`, `ft_800CEF08`, `ftCo_800CEFE0`, `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen_IASA`, `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen_Phys`, and `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen_Coll` in `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen.c`.
Added matching implementations for `ftCo_800CF280`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFall_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFall_IASA`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFall_Phys`, and `ftCo_ItemParasolFall_Coll` in `ftCo_ItemParasolFall.c`.

Added matching implementations for `ftCo_800CF3C8`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial_IASA`, `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial_Phys`, and `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial_Coll` in `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial.c`.

Added matching implementations for `ftCo_800CF4DC`, `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall_Anim`, `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall_IASA`, `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall_Phys`, and `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall_Coll` in `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall.c`.

Updated consumers such as `ftCo_Fall.c`, `ftCo_FallSpecial.c`, `ftCo_DamageFall.c`, `ftCo_Jump.c`, `ftCo_JumpAerial.c`, `ftCo_PassiveWall.c`, `ftPe_Init.c`, `ftPe_SpecialHi.c`, and `ftdata.c` to include the specific new item-parasol headers instead of `ft/ft_0CEE.h`.

Added `ftCo_ItemParasolGetFallMotionId` and `ftCo_ItemParasol_Phys` helpers in `ftCommon/inlines.h`; the motion-id helper selects Peach-specific parasol motion states when `fp->kind == FTKIND_PEACH` and `fp->fv.pe.parasol_gobj_0 != NULL`, otherwise common motion states.
Added `mv.co.parasol_open.prev_msid` to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` so ItemParasolOpen can remember the prior motion state and choose between fall and fall-special exits.
Improved `ftCommonData` typing around parasol parameters: `x58C` and `x590` became `float`, `x594` became `open_parasol_threshold`, and `x598` became `close_parasol_threshold`.
Renamed Peach fighter variables from `unk_item_gobj`/`parasol_gobj` to `parasol_gobj_0`/`parasol_gobj_1` and changed several Peach item object fields to `Item_GObj*`.
smart_moves: The split aligned source-unit names with motion-state concepts instead of keeping a generic address-range filename, making headers and includes much more searchable.
The PR linked the new units as Matching immediately, updating both `splits.txt` and `configure.py`; this avoided leaving the split as source-only bookkeeping.

Replacing `ft/ft_0CEE.h` with per-motion headers reduced accidental coupling and made dependencies explicit: files that only need the open check include `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen.h`, while damage fall users include `ftCo_ItemParasolDamageFall.h`.
The shared Peach-vs-common motion-state selection was factored into `ftCo_ItemParasolGetFallMotionId`, avoiding duplicated conditional logic in the Open and FallSpecial entry paths.

The shared parasol fall physics wrapper `ftCo_ItemParasol_Phys` preserves the old behavior where Fall and FallSpecial delegated to `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen_Phys`, while giving the call site a motion-specific name.
Adding meaningful common-data names for `open_parasol_threshold` and `close_parasol_threshold` made the decompiled IASA/open checks easier to understand without changing layout.
The diff preserved address comments in the new headers, making it clear which original functions moved from the `0x800CEE70..0x800CF594` range into each new unit.
decomp_lessons: When splitting an address-named fighter file, update all three layers together: GALE01 splits, build object list, and header include graph.

Small already-matching callbacks can be moved out of an old aggregate file into semantic motion units; decomp.dev may show them as broken in the deleted unit and newly matched in the new units, which is expected for a split.

Motion-state-specific headers help eliminate placeholder-style prototypes such as `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`; this PR replaced those with typed `Fighter_GObj*` prototypes for the new item-parasol headers.
For common fighter behavior with a character-specific override, a small inline helper can select between `ftPe_MS_*` and `ftCo_MS_*` IDs based on fighter kind and character item state.
Naming common-data fields after observed comparisons, such as open/close thresholds for control-stick Y checks, is useful once the code context proves the purpose.
Moving motion variables into `union ftCommon_MotionVars` with a named sub-struct can unlock clean matching code when callbacks need to remember previous motion state.

Clean split PRs may also prune stale includes; this one removed broad or unused includes such as `ft/ft_0CEE.h`, `un/un_2FC9.h`, `lb/lb_00CE.h`, and an unnecessary `ftCommon/forward.h` include in `ftCo_Fall.h`.
naming_conventions: New ftCommon motion files use `ftCo_ItemParasol<Motion>.c/.h` names: `Open`, `Fall`, `FallSpecial`, and `DamageFall`.
Motion callback names follow the standard suffix pattern `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll` under the motion prefix, e.g. `ftCo_ItemParasolFallSpecial_IASA`.
Address-derived entry functions remain address-named when not yet semantically named, e.g. `ftCo_800CEE70`, `ft_800CEF08`, `ftCo_800CEFE0`, `ftCo_800CF280`, `ftCo_800CF3C8`, and `ftCo_800CF4DC`.
Header guards use the semantic module name, e.g. `MELEE_FTCO_ITEMPARASOLOPEN_H`, `MELEE_FTCO_ITEMPARASOLFALL_H`, `MELEE_FTCO_ITEMPARASOLFALLSPECIAL_H`, and `MELEE_FTCO_ITEMPARASOLDAMAGEFALL_H`.
Common-data fields were improved from opaque `x594`/`x598` to behavior names `open_parasol_threshold` and `close_parasol_threshold`.

Peach item pointers were typed as `Item_GObj*` and renamed to parasol-specific names, though the numeric suffixes `parasol_gobj_0` and `parasol_gobj_1` indicate the exact roles were still only partially understood.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split the original `ft_0CEE.c` address range at function-boundary-aligned cut points: Open `0x800CEE70..0x800CF280`, Fall `0x800CF280..0x800CF3C8`, FallSpecial `0x800CF3C8..0x800CF4DC`, and DamageFall `0x800CF4DC..0x800CF594`.
Preserved `.sdata2` ranges per new unit in `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, indicating literal/data placement was part of making the split link correctly.
Used exact motion flags from the matched code, including `Ft_MF_SkipHit | Ft_MF_SkipParasol` and `Ft_MF_KeepFastFall`, in the new motion entry functions.
Introduced a local static inline `ft_800CEF08_inline1` in `ftCo_ItemParasolOpen.c` to share bitfield updates and `x2104` assignment between `ft_800CEF08` and `ftCo_800CEFE0`.
Used `RETURN_IF(...)` chains in IASA functions to match early-exit control flow for special-air, item throw, aerial catch/escape, and related interrupt checks.
Kept explicit casts to `HSD_GObjEvent` for collision callbacks where needed, such as `ftCo_LandingFallSpecial_Enter_Basic` and `ftCo_80090984`.
The bot report confirms the tactic succeeded: 22 functions became 100% matched across the four new units, with net +1472 matched bytes and +1828 linked bytes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 41.12% (+0.04%, +1472 bytes) and linked code increased to 26.28% (+0.05%, +1828 bytes).
The bot listed 22 new 100% matches in the four new item-parasol units and 12 broken matches in the deleted `main/melee/ft/ft_0CEE` unit, consistent with a file split rather than a regression.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to `ftCo_800CEE70`, `ft_800CEF08`, `ftCo_800CEFE0`, `ftCo_800CF280`, `ftCo_800CF3C8`, or `ftCo_800CF4DC`.
Check whether `parasol_gobj_0` and `parasol_gobj_1` are later renamed to roles such as active parasol item vs held/replaced item.
Search for later cleanup of `p_ftCommonData->x58C`, `x590`, `x59C`, and related parasol physics parameters.
Compare this split pattern against other ftCommon item motion splits that replace address-named files with semantic `ftCo_*` units.
Investigate the `@todo What is this copypasta...` in `ftCo_800CEFE0` for later deduplication or a more accurate role name.
